meta.note: Session 2 audit — all 19 prior items shipped. Entirely fresh item set.

items.title: Vault Heist — last-resort steal from the gold leader
items.description: When a player is reduced to 2 or fewer territories they unlock a one-time 'VAULT HEIST' command: their next convoy, if it successfully delivers to any vault site they don't control, extracts 50% of that site's accumulated gold reserves (capped at 200k). The command costs 20k gold to activate, displays a skull icon above the convoy, and is visible to all players. Creates genuine comeback tension and forces the leader to actively defend rather than coast. Nobody in the browser-RTS space has a mechanic this surgically satisfying.
items.recipe: Add VaultConvoyCommandType.VAULT_HEIST to VaultConvoyCommandExecution.ts. Add heistActivated boolean + heistConvoyId to VaultFrontExecution player state. On convoy arrival in tickConvoyArrivals(), if heist active, deduct 50% of destination site gold reserves and credit to the heist player. Broadcast HEIST_EXECUTED GameUpdateType. Render skull badge on the convoy icon in VaultFrontLayer.ts. Gate: player must have ≤2 territories + pay 20k gold.

items.title: Bounty Board — emergent coalition targeting against convoy bullies
items.description: After any player loses 3 convoys to the same opponent within a single game, the server fires a BOUNTY_BOARD event: a visible card appears for all players displaying the target player's name and a 150k gold reward for whoever intercepts their next convoy. The bounty expires after 3 successful intercepts or 300 ticks. Creates emergent kingmaker coalition dynamics without requiring any formal alliance system — just social gravity. No browser RTS has this.
items.recipe: Track convoyInterceptsBy[playerId][opponentId] counter in VaultFrontExecution. On crossing threshold 3, emit GameUpdateType.BOUNTY_BOARD_ACTIVATED with targetPlayerId and reward amount. Store active bounty in VaultFrontExecution state. On any convoy intercept matching the target, credit 150k to interceptor and decrement bountyChargesRemaining. VaultFrontLayer.ts renders BountyCard overlay at screen top-right.

items.title: Warchest Hunt — tick-500 event that crowns a gold target
items.description: At tick 500, the server announces the current gold leader as 'THE MARK'. For the next 120 seconds, all convoys going TO any of their vault sites carry double intercept gold value for rivals — and double delivery gold for any ally who manages to deliver through contested territory. The Mark player sees a crown icon; everyone else sees a crosshair. Completely resets late-game momentum, breaks runaway leaders, and rewards aggressive players who stayed in the fight.
items.recipe: In VaultFrontExecution.ts tick(), at gameTick === 500, identify the player with highest total gold delivered. Emit GameUpdateType.WARCHEST_HUNT_STARTED with markPlayerId. Apply 2× intercept multiplier to convoys targeting mark's sites for 120s. Apply 2× delivery multiplier for allies of mark. VaultFrontLayer.ts renders crown badge on mark territory + crosshair badge on opponents' view.

items.title: Vault Prophecy — AI-generated atmospheric match oracle at game start
items.description: At game start, Claude Haiku generates a cryptic, atmospheric 2-sentence prophecy for the match based on map name, player count, and active mutator. Example: 'The eastern vaults shall taste fire before the third wave. He who hoards will feed the hungry.' Display it as a styled card overlay for 8 seconds. Purely atmospheric, occasionally accidentally accurate, enormously shareable. Boosts CREATIVITY pillar (currently 0.20 — weakest signal) and gives every game a story before it starts. Zero competitors have anything like this.
items.recipe: On game start in GameStartingModal.ts, POST /api/match-prophecy with {mapName, playerCount, mutator}. Call claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 with system prompt: 'You are an ancient oracle. Generate a 2-sentence cryptic prophecy about a battle. Be poetic, vague, dramatic. Never mention specific mechanics.' Render ProphecyCard overlay (CSS dark gradient, serif font, fades after 8s). Cost: ~200 tok/match ≈ $0.00004. Enable prompt caching on system prompt.

items.title: Live Tournament Mode — weekly scheduled bracket with streamer-ready spectator
items.description: Weekly Saturday bracket tournament: 8 players, single-elimination, 3 rounds, automatic scheduling. A TournamentLobby page shows bracket state, upcoming match times, and results. The spectator camera (already built) auto-enables. A /tournament slash command in Discord announces brackets. Top-4 receive seasonal ELO bonuses. This turns the weekly mutator vote into a competitive ritual and makes VaultFront a schedulable social event — critical for word-of-mouth growth.
items.recipe: Add Tournament table to schema.sql. TournamentScheduler.ts (extends VaultSeasonScheduler) creates 8-player brackets every Saturday 8pm UTC. POST /api/tournament/register; GET /api/tournament/bracket. Add TournamentPage.ts to client with bracket visualization (SVG tree). DiscordNotifier.ts sends bracket + result announcements. Spectator mode auto-activates for finals room.

items.title: Map Events System — random mid-match events that alter game state
items.description: Every 400–600 ticks, a random map event fires from a pool of 6: VAULT_BONANZA (all site rewards 2× for 120s), SUPPLY_DISRUPTION (no convoy dispatches for 60s), INTELLIGENCE_BREACH (all enemy convoy routes revealed for 45s), GOLD_RUSH (next delivery to any site worth 3× but site resets), SIEGE_PROTOCOL (all territory expansion blocked for 90s), DIPLOMATIC_WINDOW (no combat damage for 30s — pure convoy racing). Announced via full-width banner.
items.recipe: Add MapEventScheduler to VaultFrontExecution with nextEventTick (400 + random(200)). On trigger, pick from EVENT_POOL, emit GameUpdateType.MAP_EVENT_FIRED with eventType + durationTicks. Handle each event type in relevant execution methods (tickConvoyArrivals, AttackExecution, etc.). VaultFrontLayer.ts renders event banner. Reset nextEventTick after each event.

items.title: Live Event Commentary — real-time AI sports ticker during gameplay
items.description: Subscribe to key game events (vault capture, convoy intercept, last-stand, surge activation, heist) and generate one-line commentary that scrolls along the bottom of the screen like a sports ticker. Claude Haiku generates 5–10 lines at match start based on player count and mutator; client consumes them on event triggers so no API call happens mid-game. Cost: ~300 tok/match upfront. Creates spectator energy and makes solo play feel observed and epic.
items.recipe: POST /api/match-commentary with {playerCount, mutator, mapName} at match start. Haiku returns JSON array of 10 commentary templates keyed to event types. ClientGameRunner.ts stores them. On each qualifying GameUpdateType event, dequeue matching commentary and render CommentaryTicker component (CSS marquee at bottom, fades after 4s). Prompt-cached system prompt.

items.title: Dynasty Mode — cross-season player bloodlines with inherited advantages
items.description: Players who finish a season in Gold tier or above earn a Dynasty Flag — a visual heraldic emblem displayed beside their name in all future games. Dynasty players start each game with +5k gold and a slight ELO floor. Dynasties can be 'broken' — defeating a Dynasty player in their first match earns the victor a 'Dynasty Slayer' badge and +50 bonus ELO. Creates persistent stakes across the wipe, makes veterans visible and targetable, and gives newcomers a story goal.
items.recipe: Add dynastyTier, dynastyFlag, dynastyBrokenBy fields to PlayerStatsStore. In VaultSeasonScheduler seasonal reset: players finishing Gold+ retain dynasty. Extend RankBadge.ts to show DynastyFlag overlay. In WinCheckExecution.ts, detect dynasty-vs-newcomer upset and emit DYNASTY_BROKEN event. EloRating applies +50 bonus on dynasty-break.

items.title: Cognitive Load Reduction — adaptive UI that hides irrelevant elements
items.description: The sidebar, convoy controls, and vault panels show simultaneously even when contextually irrelevant (convoy UI with no gold, vault panels when no site is in range). Implement a UIStateManager that drives show/hide of panels based on game phase, player state, and proximity. Early game: hide vault panel. No gold: dim convoy button + tooltip. Dead player: collapse all to minimal spectator view. Reduces visual noise by ~40% in key game phases.
items.recipe: Create UIStateManager.ts that subscribes to GameUpdates and player state. Expose showVaultPanel(), showConvoyControls(), isSpectatorMode() methods. Wrap panel render conditions in GameLeftSidebar.ts and HudLayout.ts with UIStateManager checks. Use CSS transitions (opacity + max-height) for smooth hide/show.

items.title: Mission Brief System — Claude-generated unique objective card per match
items.description: Each match gets a unique AI-generated secondary objective: 'Capture the Northern Vault within 400 ticks for +30 seasonal ELO', 'Deliver 3 convoys before tick 200 to activate Surge early'. Shown as a mission card overlay (8s) at match start and in a collapsed sidebar widget. Tracked server-side with bonus ELO on completion. Gives every match a unique narrative hook and rewards strategic adaptation.
items.recipe: POST /api/match-mission with {mapName, playerCount, mutator, vaultSiteCount} at match creation. Haiku returns {objectiveText, conditionType, conditionValue, bonusElo}. Store in match record. MissionBriefOverlay.ts shows at game start. Server tracks conditionType (VAULTS_CAPTURED, CONVOYS_DELIVERED, TICKS_UNHELD) in VaultFrontExecution and emits MISSION_COMPLETE when met.

items.title: Match Intel Layer — pay gold to temporarily reveal enemy convoy routes
items.description: New VaultFrontCommand: PURCHASE_INTEL (cost: 30k gold). Reveals all enemy active convoy routes on your map for 60 seconds with dotted-line overlays. A second tier: DEEP_INTEL (80k) reveals convoy gold amounts and ETA. Creates a meaningful gold sink, rewards strategic investment, and adds an information-warfare dimension to VaultFront's convoy meta. Pairs with the ghost-route system for genuine cat-and-mouse gameplay.
items.recipe: Add VaultConvoyCommandType.PURCHASE_INTEL and PURCHASE_DEEP_INTEL. Handle in VaultConvoyCommandExecution.ts: deduct gold, set intelActiveUntilTick on player state. In VaultFrontStatusUpdate, include visible enemy convoys only when intelActive. VaultFrontLayer.ts renders revealed routes as dashed overlay lines with distinct color. Intel icon in GameLeftSidebar with gold cost tooltip.

items.title: AI Coach Overlay — post-game Claude analysis of your 10 key decisions
items.description: After every match, a 'Review Your Game' button in WinModal triggers an async call to Claude Sonnet. Pass the player's top 10 decision moments (convoy dispatches, vault commands, surge uses, gold levels at key ticks). Claude returns a 3-paragraph tactical debrief: what worked, what was the costliest mistake, one concrete improvement. Displayed as a CoachPanel with expandable sections. Can be limited to 2 free reviews/day then ELO-gated. Transforms VaultFront from a game into a skill-development platform.
items.recipe: Add /api/match-coach endpoint. Client collects top 10 events from ClientGameRunner event log at match end. POST structured event log. Call claude-sonnet-4-6 with prompt-cached tactical system prompt. Stream response into CoachPanel.ts component in WinModal. Cache result in localStorage by matchId. Gate: 2/day free + Gold+ tier unlimited. Cost: ~1200 tok/review ≈ $0.006.

items.title: NPC Lore Generation — AI faction names and post-battle quotes for bot opponents
items.description: Bot opponents get procedurally generated faction names, emblems, and defeat/victory quotes by Claude Haiku. 'The Iron Quorum has fallen. Their last convoy burned at dawn.' Generated once per bot personality type and cached permanently. Transforms bot-heavy matches from sterile to cinematic. Boosts CREATIVITY pillar (0.20 — weakest signal). Entirely cosmetic — zero gameplay risk.
items.recipe: Add /api/bot-lore endpoint called once at server start per bot personality type. Haiku generates {factionName, emblem (emoji), defeatQuote, victoryQuote} for each of 4 bot types. Cache in-memory + server JSON file. BotExecution.ts surfaces bot factionName via PlayerImpl display name. WinModal shows botDefeatQuote or botVictoryQuote on match end. Cost: 400 tok × 4 types = ~$0.00032 total, permanent cache.

items.title: Live Replay Theater — post-death ghost-POV replay with annotated key moments
items.description: When a player is eliminated, instead of immediately switching to full-spectator, display a 30-second replay of their final 150 ticks from a cinematic ghost POV. Annotate the 3 most consequential moments (biggest gold swing, final convoy loss, vault site loss) with subtle highlight rings and timestamps. Then auto-transition to live spectator. Gives context, reduces frustration, and teaches strategy implicitly.
items.recipe: ClientGameRunner.ts maintains a 150-tick ring buffer of serialized game state snapshots. On player elimination, trigger ReplayTheater mode: replay the buffer at 1.5× speed using existing replay infrastructure. Annotation data: compare gold delta, convoy outcomes, and vault ownership across the buffer to identify 3 max-delta ticks. Render AnnotationRing components via GameRenderer in ghost-color theme. Auto-exit to spectator after replay.

items.title: Economic Warfare Expansion — sabotage, bribe, and trade deal commands
items.description: Three new VaultFrontCommands: SABOTAGE (cost 40k gold, adds 15% gold toll tax on target's next 3 convoys); BRIBE (cost 25k + 25k to target, delays their next dispatch 60 ticks with 50% acceptance chance); TRADE_DEAL (share 8% of your next convoy gold with target — diplomatic signal). The embargo system already exists. These complete an economic-warfare toolkit no browser RTS has attempted at this depth.
items.recipe: Add VaultConvoyCommandType.SABOTAGE, BRIBE, TRADE_DEAL. Handle in VaultConvoyCommandExecution.ts. SABOTAGE: track sabotageCharges[targetId] and apply toll in tickConvoyArrivals(). BRIBE: set dispatchDelayUntilTick on target player. TRADE_DEAL: flag next convoy for split. Render each as VaultCommandButton in GameLeftSidebar with gold cost, cooldown, and target-picker.

items.title: Mobile Touch Support — full touch-native game controls for smartphones
items.description: Browser RTS on mobile is largely untapped — the closest competitors don't support it. Implement a touch control scheme: pinch-to-zoom map, tap-to-select territory, long-press for action menu, swipe-up sidebar for quick commands. Vault command buttons scale to 48×48px tap targets. SpectatorAutoCamera already handles panning. Opens VaultFront to casual mobile sessions and dramatically expands the addressable audience.
items.recipe: Add TouchHandler.ts extending TransformHandler.ts. Map touch events to equivalent pointer events in ClientGameRunner. Detect isMobile() via navigator.maxTouchPoints. On mobile: increase HUD button size via CSS custom property --hud-tap-size: 48px. Implement LongPressMenu.ts as alternative to right-click context menu. Test with Chrome DevTools touch emulation.

items.title: Contextual Tutorial System — event-driven tips that teach without intruding
items.description: Implement a TutorialTriggerEngine that fires non-blocking tooltip cards on first occurrence of 20 key events: first vault site visible → 'Click to capture', first 50k gold → 'Dispatch a convoy', first convoy intercepted → 'Ghost routes hide your path'. Tips appear at the relevant element, fade after 6s, and never repeat. Stored in localStorage. Zero friction for experienced players.
items.recipe: TutorialTriggerEngine.ts maintains Set<string> of seenTips (localStorage). On each GameUpdate, check triggerConditions map: {FIRST_VAULT_IN_RANGE, FIRST_GOLD_THRESHOLD, FIRST_CONVOY_INTERCEPTED, ...}. When condition met and tip not seen, emit TutorialTipEvent. UILayer renders ContextualTooltip.ts anchored to the relevant element. 20 tip texts in TUTORIAL_TIPS constant. Founder can reset via ?debug=resetTips.

items.title: Adaptive Bot Personalities — 4 named strategic archetypes with distinct AI behavior
items.description: Replace the single BotExecution strategy with 4 named bot personality types: THE AGGRESSOR (vault-raid focus, high-pressure attacks), THE ECONOMIST (convoy-max, avoids direct combat), THE DIPLOMAT (alliance-seeking, betrays at tick 500), THE GHOST (ghost-route specialist, high deception ratio). Each has distinct weights on attack/convoy/defend decisions. Solo play becomes genuinely interesting — players learn matchups. Pairs with NPC lore generation.
items.recipe: Add BotPersonality enum to BotExecution.ts: AGGRESSOR | ECONOMIST | DIPLOMAT | GHOST. Assign personality at BotSpawner.spawn(). Each personality has weight object {attackWeight, convoyWeight, defendWeight, ghostRouteChance, alliancePingRate}. Route BotExecution.tick() through personality-weighted decision branches. BotSpawner randomly assigns personality + pairs with NPC lore names.

items.title: Session Insight Card — post-game play-style breakdown with percentile comparison
items.description: After each match, show a 'Your Play Style' card: Aggression (attacks/tick), Economy (gold/tick), Deception (ghost route ratio), Resilience (territory regained after losses). Each metric shows your value + percentile vs all players this week. Players discover they're a 'Ghost Economist' or 'Aggressive Defender' — shareable archetypes. Feeds into the feedback signal gap (proposals and sessions flagged weak in IGNIS).
items.recipe: PlayerStatsStore.ts tracks per-match metrics. Add computePlayStyle(matchStats) returning 4 normalized scores. Cache weekly percentile distribution (update every 15m). WinModal.ts adds InsightCard section showing radar chart (SVG, 4 axes) + archetype name + percentile badges. Share button copies card as image via native share API.

items.title: Clan Wars — weekly clan-vs-clan ELO bracket with Discord integration
items.description: Extend ClanStore with clan ELO. Weekly bracket: 8 clans, 3 rounds, best-of-3 matches per round. Clan members queue as a group; server reserves a room for the match. Results posted to Discord. Clan ELO shown in ClanModal.ts leaderboard. The guild-war mechanic that turns a community of individuals into a community of communities — strongest retention mechanic in competitive games.
items.recipe: Add clanElo, clanWins, clanLosses to ClanStore. ClanWarScheduler.ts (weekly cron) creates brackets from top 8 clans by member ELO average. ClanLobby page shows bracket. Clan members see 'War Active' banner in DesktopNavBar. Match rooms tagged with clanWarRoomId. EloRating updates clan ELO on match result. DiscordNotifier sends war results embed.

items.title: Claude Prompt Caching — 90% input token reduction on all Claude endpoints
items.description: The /api/battle-narrative, /api/match-prophecy, and /api/match-commentary system prompts are identical for every call. Enable Anthropic prompt caching (cache_control: {type: 'ephemeral'}) on all system prompt blocks. Expected reduction: ~90% on cached input tokens. At 800 tok system prompt × 100 matches/day = 72k tokens saved/day. Measurement: log cache_creation_input_tokens vs cache_read_input_tokens in the ledger.
items.recipe: In each Claude API handler, restructure Anthropic SDK call to pass system prompt as content block with cache_control: {type: 'ephemeral'}. Verify cache_read_input_tokens > 0 in second call. Log both fields to context-meter ledger via Stop-hook writer. Apply to all 3 existing endpoints + all new Claude endpoints added this session.

items.title: Color-Blind Accessible Theme — pattern-based territory differentiation for CVD players
items.description: Territory ownership colors and convoy route indicators rely on hue-only differentiation — invisible to 8% of male players. Add a color-blind mode toggle that switches to a high-contrast palette using shape+pattern differentiation (stripes, dots, hash patterns) in addition to color. Applies to TerritoryLayer.ts canvas rendering.
items.recipe: Add colorBlindMode toggle to game settings (localStorage). In TerritoryLayer.ts when colorBlindMode active, replace fill colors with Canvas createPattern() textures with distinct geometric patterns per player slot. Convoy icons use shape differentiation (circle/triangle/square) instead of color-only. Test with Chromatic Vision Simulator extension.

items.title: Tile Delta Encoding — broadcast only changed tiles per tick (60–80% bandwidth reduction)
items.description: The server broadcasts full game state each tick. Most tiles are unchanged between ticks. Implement delta encoding: server computes a diff of changed tile ownership/structure state and only broadcasts the diff. Spectators reconstruct state from a base snapshot + delta stream. Estimated bandwidth reduction: 60–80%. Critical for scaling to 50+ concurrent spectators during tournaments.
items.recipe: In GameServer.ts, maintain previousTickState per room. Compute delta = deepDiff(previousTickState, currentTickState). Broadcast delta frame instead of full state. Client GameRunner.ts applies delta to local state copy. New spectators: send full snapshot on join, then delta from that tick forward. Measure bandwidth via OtelResource metrics. Add tile_delta_encoding feature flag for gradual rollout.

items.title: Anti-Cheat Behavioral Signals — collect timing metrics and flag statistical outliers
items.description: Instrument the server to collect command inter-arrival time (mean/stddev), actions-per-tick distribution, and click coordinate entropy per match. Flag players whose timing stddev < 50ms (indicative of macro scripting) for founder review. Purely server-side, no client impact. No automated bans — flag only.
items.recipe: In VaultFrontExecution.ts handleQueuedCommands(), record commandTimestamps[] per player. At match end, compute {meanInterArrival, stddev, actionsPerTick} and write to PlayerStatsStore anti-cheat fields. Add GET /api/admin/anti-cheat-flags returning players with stddev < 50ms in last 5 matches. Gate behind admin JWT. Extend schema.sql.
